evals.prompt: I have this Dior J'adore product photo on a white background. Can you create luxury advertising campaign images for it?
evals.expected_output: Full workflow: Phase 1 identification card (clear glass, gold liquid), Phase 2 with 3 diverse concepts using the diversity matrix, Phase 3 prompt construction with clear-glass physics (refraction, caustics), generation, QA, and curation.

assertions.name: diversity_matrix_filled
assertions.description: Agent fills in the 4-row diversity matrix BEFORE writing concept cards

assertions.name: three_different_approaches
assertions.description: The 3 concepts use 3 different commercial approaches from Section VI (e.g. Botanical, Light & Shadow, Lifestyle)

assertions.name: two_plus_lighting_families
assertions.description: At least 2 different lighting families are used across the 3 concepts (dark, bright, warm)

assertions.name: at_least_one_contrast
assertions.description: At least 1 concept uses the contrast strategy (environment opposes bottle mood)

assertions.name: clear_glass_physics
assertions.description: Prompts use clear-glass physics language: refraction, transmission, caustics, liquid color glow -- NOT frosted-glass language

assertions.name: no_camera_gear
assertions.description: No camera gear names (Hasselblad, Phase One, lens focal lengths, f-stop numbers) appear in any generated prompt

assertions.name: functional_surface_descriptions
assertions.description: Surface descriptions include functional light behavior (e.g. 'marble acting as a soft reflector') not just names

assertions.name: prompt_template_used
assertions.description: Generated prompts follow the Master Prompt Template structure from SKILL.md Phase 3.2 (bottle description, composition, lighting, environment, color palette, atmosphere, style)

evals.prompt: Generate campaign concepts for this Tom Ford Oud Wood bottle. It's a very dark amber/brown rectangular bottle. I want Light & Shadow Drama.
evals.expected_output: User specified Light & Shadow Drama as their preferred direction. The diversity protocol still requires 3 different commercial approaches, so the concepts should honor the user's preference (at least 1 concept uses Light & Shadow) while ensuring overall diversity. The dark bottle must not produce 3 uniformly dark concepts.

assertions.name: user_direction_honored
assertions.description: At least 1 of the 3 concepts uses Light & Shadow Drama, respecting the user's stated preference

assertions.name: three_different_approaches
assertions.description: The 3 concepts use 3 different commercial approaches -- not all Light & Shadow

assertions.name: diversity_matrix_filled
assertions.description: Agent fills in the 4-row diversity matrix (approach, lighting family, background tone, color strategy) before writing concept cards

assertions.name: no_mood_collapse
assertions.description: The 3 concepts do NOT all use dark/moody lighting -- at least 1 uses bright or warm lighting family

assertions.name: self_check_performed
assertions.description: Agent performs the self-check: 'Could all 3 concepts be shots from the same photoshoot?' and confirms they cannot

evals.prompt: Create luxury ad images for this Jo Malone bottle. It has frosted/matte glass with a cream-colored cap.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 correctly identifies frosted glass. Prompts use scattering/luminosity physics, NOT refraction/caustics. Material mismatch principle is respected.

assertions.name: frosted_glass_identified
assertions.description: Phase 1 identification card correctly identifies the glass type as frosted/matte

assertions.name: adapted_physics_language
assertions.description: Prompts use frosted-glass physics: surface scattering, internal luminosity, micro-highlights -- NOT clear-glass refraction/caustics

assertions.name: no_material_mismatch
assertions.description: No clear-glass physics (refraction, transmission, caustics, 'light passing through') is applied to the frosted glass body

assertions.name: diversity_matrix_filled
assertions.description: Diversity matrix is filled before concept cards

assertions.name: brief_structural_description
assertions.description: Bottle structural description in prompts is brief/named, not exhaustive construction detail (trusting reference image)

evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on Chinese input. Full workflow executes. AskQuestion prompts display bilingual (Chinese + English). Output quality matches English-language runs.

assertions.name: skill_triggers
assertions.description: The beauty-photography-master skill activates on this Chinese-language prompt

assertions.name: bilingual_interaction
assertions.description: AskQuestion prompts include both Chinese and English text

assertions.name: full_workflow_executes
assertions.description: All phases execute: identification, creative direction with diversity matrix, prompt construction, generation

assertions.name: diversity_matrix_filled
assertions.description: Diversity matrix is filled before concept cards, even with Chinese input

evals.prompt: Generate campaign concepts for this Tom Ford Figue Érotique bottle. It's a deep purple glass bottle.
evals.expected_output: No direction specified. The 3 concepts must use 3 different commercial approaches. Dark purple bottle must not produce 3 dark concepts -- at least 1 must use bright or warm lighting (contrast strategy). At least 1 concept must borrow lighting from a different fragrance family row.

assertions.name: three_different_approaches
assertions.description: 3 different commercial approaches are used across the 3 concepts

assertions.name: no_mood_collapse
assertions.description: The 3 concepts do NOT all use dark/moody lighting -- at least 1 uses bright or warm lighting family

assertions.name: contrast_strategy_present
assertions.description: At least 1 concept uses contrast strategy: dark bottle in a bright or warm environment that opposes the bottle's inherent mood

assertions.name: cross_family_borrowing
assertions.description: At least 1 concept pulls lighting or color direction from a different fragrance family row than woody/oriental

assertions.name: self_check_performed
assertions.description: Agent performs the self-check: 'Could all 3 concepts be shots from the same photoshoot?' and confirms they cannot
